long_definition: The moistness of an organ which remains in equilibrium, qualitatively and quantitatively, in accordance to the temperament of that organ.

A severe type of chronic headache which involves the whole head. The patient feels that someone is hammering his head. Most of the physicians are of the view that both these types are same but Daud Antaki differentiates them by the site of pain as Bayda is a headache which occurs in the central part of head while Khudha is a headache which is experienced on the periphery of head.
A type of severe headache which involves one half of head whether it is right or left. It is an episodic type of pain accompanied with nausea and vomiting.
A type of migraine which is caused by predominance of Safra (yellow bile) and Dam (blood/sanguine) in the body.
A type of migraine which is caused by predominance of Balgham (phlegm) and Sawda (black bile) in the body.
It is a type of severe headache which occurs on the forehead above the eyebrow. It can involve either sides or both sides. The intensity of pain gradually reduces with the sunset.
The inflammation of meninges of brain or brain itself.
The inflammation of meninges of brain due to predominance of Dam (blood/sanguine).
The inflammation of meninges of brain due to predominance of Safra (yellow bile).
The inflammation of meninges of brain due to predominance of Sawda (black bile).
The inflammation of meninges of brain due to predominance of Balgham (phlegm).

short_definition: inflammation of brain or its meninges
long_definition: The inflammation of meninges or tissues of brain.
In this condition, symptom of Sarsam (meningitis) occurs without any inflammatory changes in the brain. This is caused by other reason e.g. accumulation of Bukharat (gases) in the brain.
A type of acute inflammation of brain tissues caused by accumulation of putrified blood in the brain.
A type of inflammation of the vessels of brain caused by the accumulation of Muhtariq Dam (burnt blood/sanguine) in the vessels.
A type of inflammation of brain tissues caused by the accumulation of Muhtariq Sawda (burnt black bile) or sanguine mixed with Safra (yellow bile) in the brain.

Literal meaning of Utash is excessive thirst. It is a disease which occurs commonly in children. In Unani System of Medicine, it is considered to be a sub acute type of inflammation of brain in which the child experiences an increased thirst.

Mashra is a Suryani word which literally means acute inflammation of any part of body due to predominance of Dam (blood/sanguine) and Safra (yellow bile). Ancient Unani physicians used this word for the inflammation of face and forehead. Ibn Sina included this term for the acute inflammation of liver. But most recent Unani physicians describe this as an acute inflammation of face and forehead with pain and itching.

Accumulation of fluid in the head. Can be beneath the scalp or inside the head. If inside the head, patient experiences heaviness of head, eyes remain open with continuous lacrimation and this is fatal. If beneath scalp, usually curable (caput succeedenum).
A prolonged phase of deep unconsciousness.
A type of deep sleep with phases of consciousness in between. Due to predominance of Balgham (phlegm). According to Qarshi and others, it can also occur due to Safra and Balgham.

short_definition: coma due to coldness of brain
long_definition: Caused by exposure to cold or intake of narcotic drugs.

short_definition: coma due to moistness of brain
long_definition: Caused by the predominance of Rutubat (moistness) in the body hindering oxygenation.
Caused by predominance of Dam (blood/sanguine) in the body.
Produced by gases from bad humours of stomach, lungs, intestine or uterus.

short_definition: coma due to weakness of brain
long_definition: Caused by the weakness of brain and nerves.
Due to injury of head and brain especially temporal region.
A disease in which the patient remains in wakeful state all the time.
Caused by predominance of Yubusat (dryness) either in terms of matter i.e. Sawda (black bile) and Safra (yellow bile) or simple.
Wakefulness due to accumulation of Rutubat (moistness) in the brain.
A state of forgetfulness.

short_definition: forgetfulness due to coldness and moistness/ Retrograde Amnesia
long_definition: Forgetfulness caused by predominance of Burudat (coldness) or Rutubat (moistness).

short_definition: forgetfulness due to coldness and dryness/ Anterograde Amnesia
long_definition: Forgetfulness caused by predominance of Burudat (coldness) or Yubusat (dryness).

short_definition: forgetfulness due to heat and dryness
long_definition: Forgetfulness caused by predominance of Yubusat (dryness) in brain tissues. Incurable if tissue loss present.
A confused state of mind not reaching the level of Junun. A type of melancholia.
A state in which the patient talks continuously and irrelevantly.
Weakness of thinking faculties leading to distraction. Patient cannot maintain social equilibrium and engages in irrelevant tasks.
Weakness of faculties leading to egoistic behaviour, violence, superiority feelings.
Combination of Malan (black) and Khuliya (safra). Severe depression due to burnt black bile or burnt yellow bile in the brain. Patients experience false perceptions.
Due to accumulation of Muhtariq Dam (burnt sanguine). Characterised by red eyes, strong pulse, confused mind, happiness.
Due to Muhtariq Balgham (burnt phlegm). Characterised by laziness and cold clammy skin.
Due to Muhtariq Safra (burnt yellow bile). Characterised by insanity, anger, restlessness, delirium.
Due to Muhtariq Sawda (burnt black bile) and Sawda (black bile). Characterised by fear, disturbing thoughts, crying.

Caused by Muhtariq Safra (burnt yellow bile). Chronic as humour is viscous. Patient lives in isolation, stays in graveyards by day, comes out at night, irritative, emaciated, pale, sometimes aggressive and makes noises like dog and cock.

term: Malankhuliya Marqi/ Nafkh Marqi/ Marqiyya/ Illat Nafikha/Sharsufiya
long_definition: Occurs when burnt humour accumulates in peritoneum due to heat producing Bukharat (gases) which disturb the brain.
Disturbed state of mind with hallucinations and behavioural changes. Patient becomes insane and undisciplined.
